birth.historical_notes: Born to Robert Gay Hooker Sr. + Elizabeth Lawton Shreve, likely SF or Hillsborough San Mateo County

sources.short: Tony Hooker pedigree (Robert Gay Hooker Sr. children)
sources.page: Robert Gay Hooker Jr. b.1901 d.1984

narrative_blocks.header: Managed Harvard's freshman hockey team in 1920
narrative_blocks.body: He was appointed manager of the Harvard freshman hockey team on February 11, 1920, with classmate Donald Fairfax Bush Jr. as assistant. He graduated Harvard in the class of 1923 from Burlingame, California.

narrative_blocks.header: Married daughter of American Water Works founder
narrative_blocks.body: He married Marion Sabina Kuhn (1904-2000), daughter of Pittsburgh-born industrialist William Speer Kuhn — founder of the American Water Works Company. The marriage joined two transcontinental industrial fortunes.

burial.plot_notes: Cypress Lawn Memorial Park, Colma San Mateo County CA — Garden / Section: D, Lot: 129

parents.father_research_notes: John S. Kuhn (1813-1883) of McKeesport PA — owned the local gas company in Muncie IN per American Water Works company history.

sources.short: Water Works History — American Water Works Company history
'Statistical Tables of American Water Works' (1887, p.50)

careers.notes: Founded with brother James S. Kuhn in 1882. Built and bought small water-utility companies across the East and Midwest.

careers.employer: American Water Works and Guarantee Company (Limited)
careers.notes: Formed Aug 20, 1886 as a Pennsylvania partnership with brother James S. Kuhn, Charles H. Payson, and others. The 1874 PA partnership-law structure permitted ownership of stock in subsidiary water + gas companies that corporations could not legally hold. $400,000 of paid-up capital came from stock in five Kuhn-built water companies (Connellsville PA, Wellsville NY, Sioux Falls SD, New Haven PA, Muncie IN). Built or bought 19 more water systems 1886-1891 (Wichita, Mt Vernon, Huntington WV, Chattanooga, Kokomo, Sheboygan, Keokuk, etc).

careers.employer: American Water Works and Guarantee Company (NJ corporation 1891)
careers.notes: Reorganized as NJ corporation Jan 26, 1891. Continued building and buying water systems, plus heavy investment in Western irrigation systems. Declared bankrupt 1913 due to over-extension.

notable.notable_blurb: Pittsburgh industrialist; founded American Water Works
notable.primary_url_label: Water Works History — American Water Works Company history

narrative_blocks.body: William Speer Kuhn was born November 30, 1856 in Pittsburgh, Allegheny County PA to John S. Kuhn (1813-1883), a McKeesport PA-based businessman who owned the gas company in Muncie IN. With his brother James S.

The 1891 NJ corporation continued to build and buy water systems through the 1900s, but began heavy investment in Western irrigation projects that stretched the company's finances to the breaking point — bankruptcy declared 1913. The successor companies reorganized as the American Water Works and Electric Company (Virginia 1913, then Delaware 1927). In 1936 the firm partnered with Chemical Bank & Trust Company of New York to form American Communities Company, controlling 43 water systems through Community Water Service Co. On March 19, 1946 the name changed to American Water Works Company Inc. — the modern entity that continues today as one of America's largest water utility holding companies.

narrative_blocks.header: His daughter joined San Francisco's Hooker dynasty
narrative_blocks.body: William Speer Kuhn married Tennessee-born Katherine 'Katie' Hill Kuhn (1873-1941), 17 years his junior. Their daughter Marion Sabina Kuhn (1904-2000) married Robert Gay Hooker Jr., bringing the Pittsburgh utility-fortune Kuhn line into Sam Talcott Hooker's San Francisco Hooker family. Both William and Katherine eventually relocated to California, where they died in San Mateo and were buried at Cypress Lawn Memorial Park in Colma — the same Bay Area cemetery as the Shreve and Hooker families.

burial.plot_notes: Cypress Lawn Memorial Park, Colma San Mateo County CA — same plot as husband William Speer Kuhn
